# real-browser-mcp

> MCP server + Chrome extension that gives AI agents control of your real browser with existing sessions and logins.

## What it does

real-browser-mcp is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server paired with a Chrome extension. It lets AI coding agents (Cursor, Claude, Windsurf, and any MCP client) interact with the user's actual browser - not a headless copy, not a new instance. The browser the user already has open, with their tabs, cookies, sessions, and logins intact.

## Key differentiator

Unlike Playwright MCP or Chrome DevTools MCP, which launch or attach to a separate browser, real-browser-mcp controls the browser the user is already using. For projects with authentication, complex state, or corporate SSO, this means no re-login, no session setup, no cookie recreation.

## Tools (17 total)

Navigation: browser_navigate, browser_tabs
Interaction: browser_click, browser_type, browser_press_key, browser_scroll, browser_hover, browser_select, browser_wait
Reading: browser_snapshot (compact mode default - interactive elements only), browser_screenshot, browser_text, browser_find
JavaScript: browser_evaluate (execute arbitrary JS in page context, supports async/await)
Dialogs: browser_handle_dialog (handle alert/confirm/prompt)
Debugging: browser_console, browser_network

## Architecture

- MCP server: Node.js/TypeScript, communicates with AI agent via stdio
- Chrome extension: Manifest V3, communicates with MCP server via WebSocket on localhost
- All communication is local. No cloud, no telemetry, no data exfiltration.

## Install

npm: npx real-browser-mcp
Chrome extension: Load unpacked from the extension/ directory

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/ofershap/real-browser-mcp
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/real-browser-mcp
